decision_target: smallest safe observation projection and extension change
source_of_truth: workers/dkr-3/artifacts/context-observation-probe.v1.json plus current Lite, observable, logging, and SDK session source
read_method: run the saved replay checker against the saved probe artifact
questions_answered: Current observable events already cover nested parentage, success, error, and context-owned sink flush and close.
Current observable events do not carry session, activation, work, channel, role, or tool fields; AbortError remains an error phase and context close is not an event.
Lite core already supplies wrapExec, parent links, exact named-tag lookup, and onClose, so safe projection does not require a new Lite primitive.
A named SDK observation value plus an observable extension option that reads only that tag can cover the trace without enumerating arbitrary tags.
The probe exported none of the three forbidden sentinel values from unrelated tags or session config.
Runtime-provided required tags and full declared dependency activation are accepted frame choices; the candidate adds no built-in tag injection or hidden effect edge.
questions_unanswered: Which public names should identify the SDK observation namespace and observable event context field?
Should cancellation extend Observable.Phase or use a separate terminal field?
Which declared edge allocates the activation id, and is it durable or activation-local?
Does the same projection remain isolated when two session activations share one Scope?
How should structured context cancellation and close ordering from the separate lifecycle DKR affect terminal event timing?
decision: candidate only; never accepted by this worker

confidence_probability_update: 0.55 before source and replay probes; 0.88 after the baseline and safe-projection replay
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: Enabling observable input or output remains a separate sensitive-data decision and requires redaction.
A projection callback with arbitrary ContextData access would weaken the proof; the extension should accept one named safe projection tag instead.
The current probe does not prove cross-session isolation, so that wall remains unknown and blocks downstream work.
The writer replay is not independent validation, so single-LLM truth remains breached until a validator record exists.
No Lite core projection change is justified by this probe.

candidate_ckrs.target: 12/12 required trace dimensions with forbidden export count 0
1 explicit safe projection tag and 0 arbitrary tag enumeration paths

candidate_pkrs.scope: define the SDK safe observation namespace and compose it at declared activation, work, channel, role, tool, and subagent exec edges
let observable extension read one configured projection tag, classify cancellation, and emit activation close before sink settlement
add same-Scope two-session, forbidden-value, parentage, terminal, close, sink-flush, and OTEL attribute tests
